claimText: Artificial Narrow Intelligence (ANI) specializes in a single area, whereas Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) learns continuously like humans, and Artificial Superintelligence (ASI) surpasses human cognitive abilities.
evidenceExcerpt: ANI specializes in one area; AGI learns continuously like humans; ASI surpasses human cognitive abilities.
examUse: Statement-based question comparing ANI, AGI, and ASI.
distractorHooks: Swapping the definitions of ANI and AGI
Claiming ASI is currently the standard AI used in smartphones

claimText: Supervised machine learning relies on labeled datasets, whereas unsupervised machine learning utilizes unlabeled datasets.
evidenceExcerpt: Supervised learning uses labeled datasets; Unsupervised uses unlabeled datasets.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question distinguishing between supervised and unsupervised learning.
distractorHooks: Reversing the definitions so supervised uses unlabeled data
Confusing unsupervised learning with reinforcement learning

claimText: GPT (Generative Pre-trained Transformer) is built on deep learning architecture.
evidenceExcerpt: GPT stands for Generative Pre-trained Transformer, built on deep learning architecture.
examUse: Statement-based question on the underlying technology of GPT models.
distractorHooks: Claiming GPT is based on traditional rule-based programming instead of deep learning
Expanding GPT as General Purpose Technology

claimText: Agentic AI systems can autonomously make decisions and adapt, an example of which is India's first agentic AI named Kruti.
evidenceExcerpt: Agentic AI systems autonomously make decisions and adapt (e.g., India's first agentic AI Kruti).
examUse: Fact-based or correct-pair question identifying Kruti as India's first agentic AI.
distractorHooks: Describing Kruti as a traditional chatbot without autonomous decision-making
Naming another AI as India's first agentic AI
